title: Florida (Joke)
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Florida}} __NOTOC__ {{Joke}}
{{Quote|Florida is a particularly odd level of the Backrooms. It's safe, but also dangerous at the same time. I've been here for a while, and the only advice I can give is that you'll get used to it.|Dixie Winnst, a wanderer within Florida.|color=#ffffff}} {{Class 0|color-h=2.2|color-s=100%|color-l=50.8%|class=3f - Floridian|safety=Unsafe|security=Secure|entity=Floridian Hazards|hexagon=F}} '''Florida''' is an anomalous level of the Backrooms.
----
'''Description'''
 {{Blurimage|file=Florida HOI4.png|width=256px|caption=A rough map of what Florida looks like.|hovertext=Is that Hearts of Iron 4?}} '''Florida''' is best described as an expansive landscape, primarily consisting of either sprawling urban areas or swamps. These areas are highly inhabited by various entities and barraged by various odd occurrences, though many people within the level seem to have adapted to these circumstances. This is especially proven by the fact that some wanderers tend to stay in residential areas found within these urban areas. Additionally, '''Florida''' is very well known for its high abundance of entities known as "Florida Men," which are prevalent almost everywhere in the level. Other than that, however, '''Florida''', despite mostly being swamps and towns, also contains many different areas. It is particularly known for its beaches, which are usually quite exhilarating. Otherwise, the sea near the beaches is infested by algae, causing the shores to be red. The phenomenon happens frequently at the beaches, meaning that it's rather ideal to stay away from the beaches unless you are ''positive'' there is no algae buildup in the waters. '''Florida''' is also well known for its subtropical climate, making it very hot all the time. Due to this warm climate, wanderers typically come here to try and heat up, though they are sidetracked by the various anomalies and entities present within the level. The weather in '''Florida''' is fluctuant, meaning that it can go from perfectly clear skies to thunderstorms in the matter of a few minutes. These changes in weather are incredibly common, and that, combined with the general humidity of the level, makes it unpleasant for those who simply want to pay a visit. Aside from that, traffic is also common in '''Florida''', despite wanderers rarely having access to vehicles. These are especially seen in urban areas, which makes traveling across roads or practically anywhere in urban areas extremely difficult. The reason for this traffic, however, remains unknown. Contrary to most maps, '''Florida''' is completely isolated, and the boundaries of the finite area are shaped similar to a boot. Beyond these boundaries is what seems to be an indefinitely large ocean, which also seems to contain the aforesaid beaches. It is not known if anything lies within or past the ocean other than '''Florida''' itself, as most attempts to explore the body of water have simply amounted to naught. Unlike most backrooms levels which usually contain Almond Water or some kind of "normal" water, '''Florida''' has its own kind of water named ''"Sulfur Water."'' This gives it this "raw fish" smell, and also makes it taste slightly worse. ----
'''Florida Men'''
 {{Blurimage|file=Florida Countryball.jpg|width=256px|caption=Crude depiction of a Florida Man..|hovertext=Truly the most Floridian of them all.|align=left}} Florida Men are very common entities within '''Florida''', and are known to be dangerously chaotic. Their motives are not entirely known, with some of these entities simply trying to "help" others. Despite this, they usually try to achieve these feats by doing things that would probably be considered reckless by most people. Contrary to what some believe, Florida Men do not look humanoid, but rather look like what is referred to as a "country ball." The country ball bears the flag of '''Florida''', and they often lack a mouth or nose. Despite them not having limbs, they are able to easily carry and move around objects, which is observed to make it look like the object is "hovering." Depending on the "animation style" of the Florida Man, they, on rare occasions, bear a mouth. Despite this, they seem to speak whether they have a mouth or not, and they typically talk about '''Florida''', or random goofy things. Generally, if given a car, they will drive recklessly without hesitation due to unknown reasons (possibly due to lack of cars in the backrooms). Florida Men typically make an appearance on the headlines in newspapers scattered around '''Florida''', and examples of them will be shown below: *Florida Man turns the country of Italy into an alligator. *Florida Man attempts to take selfie with faceling, ends up spontaneously combusting. *Florida Man disappears into the shadow realm. ----
'''Publix Subs'''
 {{Blurimage|file=Publix Sub.png|width=256px|caption=An info card detailing the basics of the Publix Sub.|hovertext=I made a severe and continuous lapse in my judgement, and I don’t expect to be forgiven. I’m simply here to apologize.|align=right}} Publix Subs, also referred to as "Pub Subs," are a kind of submarine sandwich that give rejuvenating effects to the person who consumes it. Because Almond Water is not present within '''Florida''', Publix Subs are typically seen as an alternative, as claimed by various people who inhabit the level. The subs are said to restore any kind of energy lost from the wanderer, as well as boost the energy the have. Furthermore, Publix Subs are said to give people who inhabit '''Florida''' an urge to continue eating more subs. Whether this is an anomalous effect or not is unknown, though such a detail is irrelevant. Publix Subs come in a nearly infinite number of variants, as people typically customize their sub by adding toppings and condiments of their choice. No matter what they put on it, it still remains a Publix Sub, including all of its effects. '''Florida''' inhabitants are well known for disliking any other ind of sub that isn't the Publix Sub, and can range from snarky remarks to a punch in the face, depending on the kind of sub you have. Additionally, the Publix Sub is the only sub in '''Florida''' to give off such effects, so you shouldn't take it for granted. ----
'''Discovery'''
 '''Florida''' was discovered by a wanderer named "Ponce De León" in the year 1513. Based off scriptures found across the Backrooms, it is said the level's name, "Florida," actually meant "flowers" in Spanish, as the level presumably had many flowers at the time. He explored much of '''Florida'''' coastline, some claiming he went as far as the panhandle. Although Ponce De León's whereabouts within the Backrooms are now unknown, most people within the level typically state that they know where he his, though never say where, for unknown reasons. ----
'''Entry and Exiting'''
 '''Florida''' is, in itself, able to be accessed from any level in the Backrooms by interacting with any kind of vacation poster advertising the level. These posters typically say something along the lines of: "Come to our beaches, which always have red tide!" It could also be accessed by following a pathway that slowly transitions into a beach within '''Florida'''. '''Florida''' is able to be exited by climbing into a manhole found within the road, which will send one to [[The Schimmelplatz]]. Furthermore, eating a Publix Sub might rarely cause one to no clip through the floor, which would send someone to a completely random level.
